---
name: project-gain-agent-challenge
description: "Rob's team is competing in Northwestern's GAIN Agentic Investigation Challenge — lobbying + press-release corpus; deadline 2026-07-15; repo at github.com/rcalvey05/gain-investigation"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: cd49f948-406c-4612-965e-ec1161d17305
---

Rob is on a team entering the **GAIN Agentic Investigation Challenge** (gain-agent-challenge.northwestern.edu). **Deadline 2026-07-15.**

Where everything lives (all state is externalized — read these, don't rely on conversation memory):
- `C:\Users\rcalv\Projects\Northwestern Project\CLAUDE.md` — project orientation
- `...\Northwestern Project\_Plan.md` — plan v2.1 (QA'd by 5 cold-reader agents), decision register
- `...\Northwestern Project\gain-investigation\` — git repo, pushed to **github.com/rcalvey05/gain-investigation** (private). Its CLAUDE.md has working conventions + "data facts that bite" (real schema quirks, the dedup-before-summing rule). LEDGER.md has the leads; DECISIONS.md the human-judgment log.
- Raw corpus: `...\Northwestern Project\data\data\` (house 2025 Q1–Q3 + Registrations still downloading as of 2026-07-04).

Status as of 2026-07-05 (test-pass deep dives done, commits through db21a98): Rob triaged L004+L003 (DECISIONS.md). L004 killed as naming artifact (press says "Farm Bill"/"NDAA", filings cite numbers; bills were Farm Bill/THUD approps/NDAA) → spawned **L010: DIPRA pipe war** ($540K/qtr via Bradley Arant + ex-Rep. Martha Roby vs fragmented plastics coalition; needs 2025 baseline from pilot build). L003 all four decoded, now investigating: **Innovairrs (Pakistan client, $1M round-number quarter to Checkmate, zero gov entities on LD-2 — LDA-vs-FARA legal-flag question awaiting Rob)**; Fay Law ($840K = 80% of Jake Perry's book, USVSST Fund); Five Rivers ($495K real, IRS "collections matters", easement profile); Coretsu ($760K/6 firms selling AI to policymakers). New data rules in repo CLAUDE.md: never sum senate+house (chamber copies), house dumps are partial snapshots, bill alias matching needed. **Pending: Rob's legal-flag call on Innovairrs; full pilot build when house 2025 finishes downloading; COI statements; team split.**

Status as of 2026-07-06 (generation pass, commit 6f5c5e3): LEDGER.md/DECISIONS.md were reset a 2nd time (pilot + first-sweep leads now archived — L010/L003/Innovairrs are NOT in the active ledger). Ran a fresh full-corpus generation pass with self-designed lenses (`queries/emergence_and_flows.sql`: emergence, per-client fan-out, individuals-as-clients, single-qtr spikes, LD-203 honoree concentration) — no `sweep_2026.sql` replay. Logged **L020-L025** (active ledger): L021 **Fred Daibes $1M for "Executive relief"/clemency, lobbyist = Keith Schiller (ex-Trump aide)**; L023 **Vantive $2.5M to White House Ballroom Project + six-firm standup**; L020 TP-Link ban-defence escalation; L022 Scott Sheffield (individual) lobbying "the FTC"; L024 BATT opaque battery coalition; L025 (parked) single-qtr income-overstatement data-quality class. **Pending: Rob's editorial/legal-sensitivity flag on L021 + L023** (living individuals / sitting-President's project) before any lock.

Status as of 2026-07-06 (press-issue-coupling session, commit 35a49bd on branch **feature/press-issue-frequency** — NOT yet merged to main; parallel sessions may exist). Built the press-side issue tagger: extended build_db.py with `press_issue_mentions` (ALI issue codes tagged onto press text via documented `ISSUE_KEYWORDS`, 44 codes) + `v_press_issue_quarter` view + `backfill_press_issues.py` (populates existing DB without a full rebuild). Tested temporal lobbying-messaging coupling (`queries/press_issue_coupling.sql` + `.md` write-up) on ATTENTION-SHARE vs MONEY-SHARE (raw counts confounded by ~4x press-corpus growth). Logged **L026** (primary): **Medicare/Medicaid say-vs-pay divergence** — press attention doubles in 2025 (Dem leadership vs reconciliation Medicaid cuts) while lobbying-money share falls (r_concurrent −0.72); named actors both sides, no legal flag needed (public officials, public statements). **L027** (parked): trade/tariffs coupling (r +0.88), the obvious 2025 story. **Correction to prior note:** raw corpus IS materialized on this box at `...\Northwestern Project\data\data\` — `show_record.py` press + filing_uuid round-trips worked this session (the earlier "data/ not materialized" note is stale).

Status as of 2026-07-07 (consolidation + P1 build; commit 77b5eb5 on **main**). **Everything is now on `main` — no more feature branches.** The `feature/press-issue-frequency` work (L026/L027, press_issue_coupling, backfill_press_issues, corpus_additions_roadmap) was merged to main, and a parallel chat's **`source-document-reader` skill split** (ocr_pdf.py moved out of finding-verifier into its own reusable OCR skill; finding-verifier now delegates) is also on main → repo now ships **7 skills**. **Workflow decision: Rob is the only writer this week → commit directly to main, small and often; feature branches caused a concurrent-push mess and add no value solo.** Raw-corpus state on this box: `data/` currently holds only SEC files (no senate/house/press raw tree), so `show_record.py` round-trips are blocked until it's re-materialized; but **`db/lda_full.duckdb` (1.9 GB, 2022–2026) IS present**, so DB/aggregate work runs fine.

Plan §9 now houses a **story-bucket-driven tool roadmap (P1–P7)** from Chris's input (`Story Buckets.md` = 3 story types + 3 data-integrity notes), prioritized against the data_manual "starting points". A **5-agent QA of the plan against the official rubric** (Stage-1 findings + the 4 Stage-2 dims) flagged: zero locked findings, the two ledger resets orphaned L010's trail (no ledger/INDEX/DECISIONS row), empty README §4/§5 (outside-tools + COI), and lobbyR disclosure needed. **Rob's call: teammates own the findings; my lane is the tooling.**

**P1 BUILT** (rollup double-count fix = cleanup-note A): `v_client_canonical_spend` view in `lda-entity-resolver` (+ `queries/p1_canonical_spend.sql` = cited demos/QA). Per (client, quarter) `canonical_spend = greatest(in-house amount, outside amount)`, NEVER the sum — a client that self-files in-house (registrant norm_key == client norm_key) reports its total, which already subsumes outside firms' income. Row `amount = greatest(income, expenses)` (some in-house filers report under `income`, e.g. AIPAC, so keying on expenses alone zeroed them). Validated on full corpus: **2025 naive $6.23B vs canonical $5.46B (12.3% inflation removed)**. Lead QA (ran the leads through it per Rob's OK): **L023 Vantive self-files → its spend is overstated ~$120–240k/qtr; L020 TP-Link + L024 BATT have no in-house filing → unaffected.** lobbyR `flag_client_registrant_conflict()`/`flag_dupes()` = prior art / independent cross-check, re-implemented ourselves (not copied) → disclose in README §4 + COI note.

**P2 BUILT 2026-07-07 (commit 9d40cb5 on main; fixes cleanup-note C for bills + the L004 gap).** Bill cross-check packaged into lead-scanner (skeleton removed, scripts/ no longer empty): `skills/lead-scanner/scripts/bill_lookup.py` (bill NUMBER or named ALIAS → every senate/house filing + press release touching it, each with a show_record.py key) + `bill_aliases.json` (12-bill curated/versioned/cited name↔number crosswalk) + `queries/p2_bill_crosscheck.sql` (P2a–P2d citeable blocks). Design: number side = exact `bill_mentions`; name side = whole-word regex on press text + filing free-text. Crosswalk keeps **names vs match-phrases separate** (ambiguous acronyms IRA / bare "PACT Act" are display-only) and leaves low reused numbers (H.R.1/H.R.2) **phrase-primary** since `bill_mentions` has no Congress field (Farm Bill lives here); tool warns when a number spans >1 Congress. Acceptance passed: HR5376 and "Inflation Reduction Act" resolve identically, 3 records/dataset round-trip via `show_record.py --data-root ../data/data`. Headline demo: IRA press = **53 releases cite the number vs 3,157 that name it and never cite it** (the say-vs-pay gap). New candidate **L028** (SECURE 2.0 quietly lobbied by retirement/annuity industry — Athene/Fidelity/American Benefits Council — while press near-silent; `open`, not triaged; clean signal is free-text name match, NOT the omnibus HR2617 number). **Design deviation logged:** kept `run_sweep.py` in `queries/` (didn't move into the skill as §9 suggested) — it's the citation anchor for every finding's aggregate SQL; moving it would break historical trace/finding command strings. **Correction to line 24:** raw tree IS at parent `..\data\data\`, so show_record round-trips work (use `--data-root ../data/data`); only the repo-local `data/` is SEC-only. **Next tooling: P3 — Quarterly Turnover/Termination Tracker (needs P1 for dollar deltas).**

**Why:** multi-session competition work under deadline.
**How to apply:** start sessions by reading the repo's CLAUDE.md + LEDGER.md + DECISIONS.md tail; follow the session discipline there (update ledger, export trace, commit); never cite the DB — raw records via show_record.py.
